title: Human verification workflow: every extracted PMC-OA assertion verified against cited span before card inclusion; enriched cards re-reviewed
context: The literature extraction pipeline (012) produces LLM-drafted candidate evidence statements that must be human-verified before entering any card. This task covers the verification workflow itself: curators check each extracted statement against its cited span, reject any unverifiable or fabricated statements, and record their decisions. Enriched cards must re-pass the compliance gate and receive renewed scientific sign-off before release. This depends on the assembled base cards (010) and the litextract pipeline (012).
objective: Define and execute the human verification workflow for PMC-OA extracted assertions: curators verify every LLM-drafted statement against its cited text span in the source article, accept only statements that are faithfully derived from open-licence (CC-BY/CC0) text, reject any unverifiable or fabricated statements, and record acceptance/rejection with rationale. Enriched cards re-pass the compliance gate and re-receive version-scoped scientific sign-off before any release.
acceptanceCriteria: A documented human verification workflow is committed to the repository describing how a curator reviews each extracted statement, verifies it against its cited text span in the source article, and records accept/reject with rationale
Every LLM-drafted extracted statement is individually verified by a human curator before it is accepted into a card; no unverified statement enters any released card

Fabricated or unverifiable statements (those without a faithfully citable span) are rejected and their rejection is recorded; the LLM error rate (fraction rejected) is tracked and reported per release run
NC/ND article citations (citation-only, no extracted text) are confirmed to contain no quoted or paraphrased text from the NC/ND source
Cards enriched with verified PMC-OA assertions re-pass the compliance gate and schema validation in CI
Enriched cards receive renewed version-scoped scientific-reviewer sign-off before release (referencing the updated card version and source manifest)
output: A committed human verification workflow document and verification records for each PMC-OA extracted assertion accepted into a card, with enriched cards re-passing the compliance gate and receiving renewed scientific sign-off — completing the M3 literature-enrichment cycle.
